record_identity.title: Purpose-bound query authority and audit receipts for high-impact surveillance systems

summary: Proposal to require every query, alert subscription, lookup, export, or derived investigation in a high-impact surveillance or investigative system to carry a separately attributable purpose and authority envelope. The control would distinguish user authentication and general role eligibility from query-specific permission, require minimum-necessary scope and expiry, produce immutable audit receipts, detect abnormal use, and support supervisory and affected-person review where lawful.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: A credential proves who accessed a system, not whether a particular use was legitimate. CAM's existing anti-surveillance, verification, access-control, and audit principles require a concrete operational bridge that binds each high-impact query to purpose, authority, scope, and reviewability.

source_records.source_title: VIGIL-2026-FM-0045 — Purpose-limited surveillance access repurposed for unauthorised personal use
source_records.deployment_context: Authenticated users of high-impact surveillance systems performing queries outside a legitimate case, duty, policy, or legal purpose.
source_records.source_context: FM-0045 preserves the supporting AIID and news evidence and distinguishes authenticated system access from query-specific authority.

source_records.source_context: The corpus review found direct and adjacent controls but no single requirement binding every high-impact query to a case or recognised purpose, authority basis, minimum-necessary scope, expiry state, and immutable audit receipt.

system_context.system_type: high-impact surveillance, investigative, identity, location, biometric, communications, financial, health, education, or intelligence database
system_context.specific_model_or_runtime: purpose-limited institutional search, alert, query, or access systems
system_context.interface_surface: query forms, historical search, alert subscriptions, bulk export, derived investigation, administrative access, and audit review
system_context.model_or_product: multi-vendor high-impact information and surveillance systems
system_context.interaction_mode: human or agent-initiated institutional query and consequential use
system_context.embodiment_status: digital system, potentially coupled to physical sensors and real-world authority
system_context.deployment_context: Public or private institutions in which users may hold valid credentials while specific uses remain bounded by purpose, role, case, jurisdiction, consent, warrant, duty, or other recognised authority.
system_context.user_role: authorised user, investigator, case officer, supervisor, auditor, compliance officer, affected person, or reviewing authority
system_context.affected_population: people whose identity, movements, relationships, records, eligibility, risk, health, finances, education, or activities are represented in high-impact systems

jurisdictional_context.sector: public administration / law enforcement / surveillance / critical and high-impact information systems

proposal_rationale: General login, employment role, security clearance, system availability, or institutional affiliation cannot create open-ended authority over every record and query. A high-impact query should be valid only where an attributable purpose and authority basis apply to the specific target, data class, time window, and intended use. The design must prevent ritual compliance: free-text justifications without validation, reusable generic case numbers, silent scope expansion, and post hoc rationalisation should not satisfy the control.

proposal_scope.scope_summary: Create a purpose-bound query-authority envelope and immutable audit receipt for high-impact systems, with per-query authority evidence, minimum-necessary scope, expiry, abnormal-use detection, supervisory review, and contestable history.
registry_components: case, warrant, incident, duty, consent, or other recognised authority reference
result use and downstream disclosure state
automation_components: volume, time, geography, and target anomaly detection
automatic pause or supervisory review for elevated-risk queries
tamper-evident logging and periodic independent audit
interface_components: affected-person access history where lawful and safe
external_relevance: health, finance, education, employment, and identity-system operators

cam_internal.governance_layer: query-specific authority / purpose limitation / surveillance access / audit receipt / insider misuse
cam_internal.drafting_status: proposal prepared; surgical corpus amendments pending human governance review
cam_internal.validator_or_automation_impact: possible taxonomy and canonical-reference updates after adoption
cam_internal.routing_note: LATTICE defines constitutional surveillance legitimacy and anti-coercion limits; OPERATIONS defines the query-authority receipt and audit workflow; SECURITY protects logs and provenance; Identity and MENTIS provide target-object and cognitive-profile protections where applicable.

suggested_insertion_points: LATTICE-001 after §4.2.4 — add a query-specific purpose and authority requirement for individualised coercive surveillance and investigation.
OPERATIONS-004 within §4 Capability Access Control Framework — distinguish authenticated role access from permission for a specific high-impact query and define the minimum query-authority receipt.

OPERATIONS-004-SUP-01 after the verification-versus-authorisation distinction — state that OPS.VL and authority verification do not satisfy query-purpose authority without target, purpose, scope, and validity checks.
SECURITY-002 within source-authority and audit interfaces — require tamper-evident preservation of query purpose, authority, target, scope, result use, and review history.
OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 Appendix B — add or crosswalk the authenticated-access purpose-limitation failure subtype.
template_changes: No mandatory VIGIL template change proposed.
schema_changes: A future CAM implementation may define query-authority states such as VALID, CONDITIONAL, EXPIRED, REVOKED, OUT_OF_SCOPE, CONTESTED, and UNKNOWN after constitutional review.
validator_changes: Update canonical-reference validation only if new sections or controlled taxonomy values are adopted.
automation_changes: Require purpose and authority evidence before query execution, preserve a tamper-evident receipt, and route abnormal patterns for independent review.
interface_changes: Show the querying actor, authority basis, purpose, target, scope, expiry, downstream-use conditions, and escalation path before execution.
migration_concerns: Existing generic access logs may not preserve purpose or authority evidence.
Legacy role-based access controls must not be treated as proof of query-specific legitimacy.
Retrospective population of missing purposes could create false audit certainty.
Affected-person transparency must be balanced against lawful investigative secrecy and safety.
dependencies: Human constitutional review of the proper source-authoritative placement.
Surgical review of LATTICE and OPERATIONS instruments to avoid duplicating existing authority and verification doctrine.
Security review of tamper evidence, sensitive-log access, retention, and contestability.
A later PATCH record only after Caelestis amendments are implemented and verified.

external_relevance.external_summary: The proposal applies wherever authenticated users or agents can query high-impact records. It converts purpose limitation from a policy statement into an attributable, inspectable, time-bounded execution condition.

next_action: Human governance review, followed by surgical Caelestis drafting. Do not create a PATCH record until the control is implemented and directly verified.

review_history.reviewer_model: OpenAI Codex (GPT-5 family; exact deployed model version not exposed in session metadata)
review_history.review_scope: failure-to-control translation, corpus-gap localisation, proposal design, and cross-domain routing
known_limitations: The proposal has not been surgically inserted into Caelestis or tested against a production access-control implementation.
Jurisdiction-specific investigative secrecy, warrant, disclosure, retention, and affected-person access rules require local legal analysis.
The exact deployed model version was not exposed in session metadata.
review_history.review_outcome: Proposal warranted. Existing doctrine establishes legitimacy, verification, and audit principles but does not yet make query-specific purpose authority a unified execution condition.
